headline: Notre Dame grinds past Colorado behind Smothers' heavy workload
body: Notre Dame's offense looked content to lean on its ground game in the second half, and Hollywood Smothers answered with 124 yards on 22 carries to power the Irish to a 28-19 victory over Colorado. While Smothers earned Player of the Game honors, the real story was Notre Dame's willingness to abandon the passing attack—quarterback Carr managed just 193 yards through the air—and trust their front five to impose will on Colorado's defense.

Colorado's Anderson kept his team alive through the air with 328 yards and a touchdown to Payne, who hauled in 96 yards on three receptions. The passing game produced explosive plays, but Colorado's rushing attack—58 yards total—never materialized into sustained drives. Van Horne found the end zone late, but by then Notre Dame had already seized control of field position and clock management.

The turning point arrived in the third quarter when Notre Dame simply stopped trying to beat Colorado through the air and started running downhill. Colorado's defense, geared to stop the pass, couldn't adjust quickly enough to stack the box and limit Smothers' effectiveness. The Irish controlled time of possession and limited Colorado's opportunities to put points on the board, a classic formula that proved decisive when margin for error was thin.
turningPoint: Notre Dame abandoned its balanced attack in the third quarter and rode Smothers into the ground game, forcing Colorado to make reactive adjustments that came too late.

unsungHero.name: Lucas
unsungHero.why: With 9 tackles, Lucas anchored Notre Dame's defense and set the tone for contain despite Colorado's aerial attack, yet gets overshadowed by Smothers' offensive showcase.
